title: Self-repair slice 1: renames heal exact-path memory bindings automatically on sync
context: Renaming a file silently rotted every decision related_files entry, tripwire scope, and constraint scope that pointed at the old path — the top memory-goes-stale failure mode. Phase 5 (§59.3 D) requires a repair engine; §59.5's rule: safe repairs auto-apply as revertable moves, only unsafe ones ask.
decision: src/core/repair.ts is a pure planner with ZERO guessing: git's own rename detection (commitChanges, -M) is the only old→new source, and only EXACT path matches are rewritten — globs, prefixes, and anything ambiguous are left alone (deleted paths remain the drift detector's job). Bindings covered: Decision.related_files, RejectedTripwire.scope, Constraint.scope; live records only (superseded/rejected/retired history stays as written). The sync path runs it automatically per synced commit (fail-open: a repair error never takes capture down), auto-committing each touched home as a `repair` move on the memory timeline (new memorylog kind, wrench icon in the panel) — background, one-click revertable. hunch repair [sha] is the manual surface, dry-run by default.
consequences: Renames no longer rot memory bindings — the graph self-heals as part of the normal commit flow.
Repairs are ordinary memory moves: visible on the spine, revertable, never pushed on their own.
Policy-IR binding repair + re-prove is the deliberate NEXT slice — a repaired policy must not keep a stale proof.
alternatives_rejected: Fuzzy/semantic rename matching (rejected: a wrong auto-rewrite corrupts the source of truth; the planner only trusts git's -M detection and exact matches — zero guessing is the whole safety argument)
Repair globs by prefix rewriting (rejected: a glob's intent is scope, not a file identity; prefix rewrites can silently widen or narrow enforcement)
A repair review queue (rejected: §59.5.3 — safe repairs must be invisible; only genuinely ambiguous cases may ever ask, and slice 1 simply excludes them from the plan)
